_comment: Spoken-word narration. Short complete sentences, natural breaks + contractions (how people actually talk), no trailing participle clauses or comma pile-ups, numbers spelled out. Cloned from reference.wav via fp16.

sections.text: Every morning, a baker asks the same question. How much should I bake today? Bake too much, and it goes in the bin. Bake too little, and customers leave with nothing.

BakerySense gives you the answer. Open the app, and it tells you exactly how much to bake. Today, that's ninety croissants. Not a hundred and twenty. It even shows how much you'd waste, and how likely you are to sell out.
Getting started is easy. Just upload your sales, or connect your till. Give it two weeks of history, and it learns how your shop works.
But here's the clever part. It keeps getting better on its own. Every night, it looks back at what it predicted. Then it compares that to what really sold.
Let's say it kept making too many croissants on Wednesdays. It notices the pattern. So it suggests a fix. And it tests that fix on your past sales first.
You just tap approve. And tomorrow's plan gets a little smarter. You're always the one in control.
And every shop is different. So each one learns its own rhythm. This one learned Wednesdays. That one learned Sundays.
That's BakerySense. It doesn't just guess what to bake. It learns your bakery. And it gets better every single day.
